articles.title: Türkiye 2027'de milli roketle Ay'a ulaşmayı hedefliyor
articles.title_tr: Türkiye 2027'de milli roketle Ay'a ulaşmayı hedefliyor
articles.summary: Sanayi ve Teknoloji Bakanı Mehmet Fatih Kacır, Türkiye'nin 2027 yılında milli hibrit roket motorunu ateşleyerek Ay'a ulaşacağını açıkladı. Kacır, Türkiye'nin kendi roketiyle uydu fırlatabilen ülkeler arasına girmeye yaklaştığını ve Somali'de uzay limanı inşasının sürdüğünü söyledi.

articles.title: VakıfBank'tan 2026'nın ilk çeyreğinde 15 milyar lira net kâr
articles.title_tr: VakıfBank'tan 2026'nın ilk çeyreğinde 15 milyar lira net kâr
articles.summary: VakıfBank, 2026 yılının ilk çeyreğinde 15 milyar lira net kar elde ederken, aktif büyüklüğünü yıllık yüzde 32,5 artışla 5,5 trilyon liranın üzerine taşıdı. Bu dönemde ekonomiye sağladığı toplam nakdi ve gayri nakdi finansman desteği 4 trilyon lirayı aşan banka, öz kaynaklarını da yüzde 42,3 artırarak sermaye yapısını güçlendirmeye devam etti.

articles.title_tr: Trump'tan İran mesajı: Nükleer silaha kesinlikle izin vermeyeceğiz
articles.summary: ABD Başkanı Donald Trump, İran’ın nükleer programına dair sert mesajlar vererek, Tahran’ın ABD ile anlaşma yapmaya oldukça istekli olduğunu savundu. Beyaz Saray’da konuşan Trump, ekonomik ablukanın sonuç verdiğini belirterek, "Bizim için tatmin edici bir anlaşma yapıp yapamayacağımızı göreceğiz" dedi.

articles.title: Yılmaz: Türkiye ekonomisi güçlü dayanıklılık gösteriyor, milli gelir 1,6 trilyon dolara ulaştı
articles.title_tr: Yılmaz: Türkiye ekonomisi güçlü dayanıklılık gösteriyor, milli gelir 1,6 trilyon dolara ulaştı
articles.summary: Cumhurbaşkanı Yardımcısı Cevdet Yılmaz, küresel belirsizliklere rağmen Türkiye ekonomisinin güçlü bir dayanıklılık ortaya koyduğunu belirterek, "Milli gelirimiz 2025 yılında 1,6 trilyon dolara ulaştı, kişi başına gelirimiz ise 18 bin doların üzerine çıktı" dedi. Yılmaz, Türkiye'nin 'güvenli liman' konumunda olduğunu vurguladı.

articles.title: How Reality Fracture Will Push The Boundaries Of Magic The Gathering (And Its Printers)
articles.title_tr: How Reality Fracture Will Push The Boundaries Of Magic The Gathering (And Its Printers)
articles.summary: One of Magic the Gathering's biggest releases of 2026, Reality Fracture, will see the franchise's iconic Planeswalker Jace create the Echoverse, a mirrored version of the current MTG universe in which world-defining events have been rewritten. In a press Q&A at this year's MagicCon Vegas, Magic head designer Mark Rosewater said that a "What If" set has been one of the most requested by fans in the franchise's history, and Reality Fracture satisfies that need. However, while the Echoverse creates a ton of possibilities, this release will intentionally be very focused."Very early on, we were talking about maybe going broader," Rosewater said. "But we decided that one of the lessons from March of the Machines was we went a little bit too broad, and we decided we wanted to focus a little bit more. So, we'll see nods about [other] places, but really, the story is on Hexhaven."Hexhaven is Reality Fracture's version of Strixhaven, which was the focus of the latest Magic release Secrets of Strixhaven. Typically, when one corner of the MTG multiverse is explored in a set, the subsequent releases will revolve around a completely different part of the lore, making Wizard of the Coast's choice to return to the college an unusual one for the game; this decision could easily risk inundating players with too much scholarly content. However, Rosewater reassured me that while Hexhaven is central to Reality Fracture, this set will be different from them doing a Hexhaven-focused set.Continue Reading at GameSpot

articles.title: Mortal Kombat 2 Reviews Are Split Down The Middle, And For Once That's Literal
articles.title_tr: Mortal Kombat 2 Reviews Are Split Down The Middle, And For Once That's Literal
articles.summary: After a lengthy wait and a last-minute delay, Mortal Kombat 2 is finally bicycle-kicking its way into theaters this week. The sequel to 2021's Mortal Kombat has been promising bigger fights, deeper lore dives, and Karl Urban hamming it up as fan-favorite character Johnny Cage, but what do the early reviews say? Currently, they're more divided than one of Kung Lao's opponents after they've been subjected to his fatality.GameSpot's sister site Metacritic has begun gathering up reviews, and so far, the metascore aggregate is sitting on a dead-average 50 from 21 sites at the time of writing. This number will change as more reviews go live over the next few weeks, but it looks like opinions are all over the place when it comes to Mortal Kombat 2.When it comes to positive reviews, IGN called Mortal Kombat 2 a showcase of "big and loud" action, while SlashFilm described the sequel as one of the biggest surprises of the year. On the opposite end of the review spectrum, outlets like The AV Club described it as a campy mess without any heart, Radio Times labeled Mortal Kombat 2 as a serviceable follow-up at best, and Variety says the movie is all style without any real substance. The lack of a cohesive story appears to be one of the major complaints from reviewers, but Urban's portrayal as a washed-up martial arts movie star drafted into protecting the realms has been generally well-received.Continue Reading at GameSpot

Currently, they're more divided than one of Kung Lao's opponents after they've been subjected to his fatality.GameSpot's sister site Metacritic has begun gathering up reviews, and so

articles.title: Mina The Hollower Finally Has A Release Date, At Long Last
articles.title_tr: Mina The Hollower Finally Has A Release Date, At Long Last
articles.summary: Four years ago, Yacht Club Games announced Mina The Hollower, a retro pixel-art adventure game starring a cute little mouse in a hellish world of monsters. Evoking the style of old Game Boy Legend of Zelda games, Mina quickly grew to become one of the most highly anticipated indie titles in gaming. Today, Yacht Club Games finally confirmed what those fans have longed to hear: After multiple delays, Mina The Hollower has a release date of May 29--a mere 23 days away--and a price tag of $20.Yacht Club made the announcement on social media, delivering both the release date and price via a post and a small teaser video. "Prepare yourself for one of the greatest top-down adventures ever to be delivered directly to your soul," the post read, "for less than 20 bucks."TRUE FINAL RELEASE DATE!! 🐭🙌 Mina the Hollower launches May 29 for only $19.99!Prepare yourself for one of the greatest top-down adventures ever to be delivered directly to your soul for less than 20 bucks.Mark your calendars. Tell your friends. Wishlist now - links below!🧵 pic.twitter.com/exCI1cFwSg  — Yacht Club Games (@YachtClubGames) May 6, 2026              Mina the Hollower follows the titular Mina as she's shipwrecked on an island filled with ghosts, goblins, and other monsters. Players will be able to choose between multiple weapons and abilities as they proceed through Mina's story, all while trying to rescue the cursed island she finds herself on.Continue Reading at GameSpot

articles.title: Diablo In Diablo 4? It Could Still Be A While (Or Possibly Never)
articles.title_tr: Diablo In Diablo 4? It Could Still Be A While (Or Possibly Never)
articles.summary: Diablo 4's Lord of Hatred expansion brings a conclusive end to the storyline started in the base game of Blizzard's live-service ARPG, but already players are wondering what comes next. According to those following Diablo 4's lore, it might not be Diablo himself.Spoilers for Diablo 4: Lord of Hatred below.Mephisto's defeat in the expansion brings an end to the Age of Hatred, wrapping up the storyline that started in 2023 with Diablo 4's release. Lilith is gone for the time being, having been defeated in the base game, and her connection to the player-character severed in Lord of Hatred. Mephisto is sent into the Void, from which he can't reform in hell.          That leaves a bit of a power vacuum in Sanctuary, and while many fans would likely love to see the franchise's namesake appear as the next big bad of Diablo 4, the more likely candidate is Baal, the Lord of Destruction.Continue Reading at GameSpot

articles.title: Remember 1980s Arcade Shooters? They're Back, In Roguelite Form
articles.title_tr: Remember 1980s Arcade Shooters? They're Back, In Roguelite Form
articles.summary: There's a reason games from smaller developers have increasingly embraced roguelite mechanics. The repetitive core progression allows lots of gameplay to be wrung out of the same or similar level structures while still giving players the feeling of progression and empowerment. Huntdown: Overtime, coming soon in early access, is the latest to graft roguelite hooks onto a familiar genre. This time, it's a new take on the classic arcade run-and-gun shooter. And similarly to Absolum, the combination works well, even if I am admittedly starting to feel some roguelite fatigue.Huntdown: Overtime is a sequel to 2020's Huntdown, a stylish dystopian shooter modeled after classic arcade shooters like Contra, ESWAT, or 1988's Robocop. Huntdown used a traditional level-based design, which makes an easy comparison between the two. I may still prefer the original approach for this genre, but the homage to arcades softens its more repetitive nature. Whereas plunking in quarters to play the first stage or two over and over was commonplace in arcades, it doesn't feel all that dissimilar in a roguelite.          Like the original, Overtime takes place in a gang-infested, neon-soaked hellhole of a future. It's a vision of the future where all of the big hair and fashion excesses of the 1980s got even more amped up. As a child of the '80s, this is very effective on me: a kid who thought of "gangs" as groups of ruffians with gaudy themes and baseball bats. Overtime nails this aesthetic not only in its visual style, but also in the way it captures this vision of the future that was actually presented in arcade games in that era. Faceless, pixelated mobs of goons prowl the streets, but this time they're accented by a vast array of animations that make them feel more reactive. At one point I kicked a thug away, only to have their body half-hang limply off a fire escape. That attention to detail is a lot more than I had come to expect from a shooter like this and shows a clear focus on modernization.Continue Reading at GameSpot
